channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: The Psalter:Psalm 118
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: The Psalter:Psalm 118

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-10-27
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:01: Blessed are the blameless in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord. What is the way?
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:01: Blessed are the blameless in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord. What is the way?

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-11-03
item.description: :The Psalter. Commentary on Psalm 118. Verse 2
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Commentary on Psalm 118. Verse 2

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-11-10
item.description:
:The Psalter. Commentary on Psalm 118, Verse 3. The reason for EVERY problem we have. What are the "ways" of God?
Notes for this passage are found here in word or pdf format
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary:
:The Psalter. Commentary on Psalm 118, Verse 3. The reason for EVERY problem we have. What are the "ways" of God?
Notes for this passage are found here in word or pdf format

item.title: The Psalter, 2014-11-01
item.description: :The Psalter. Personal commentary on Psalm 118:16. We must learn something from the things we read! Meditation is an active pursuit, and not just memory.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Personal commentary on Psalm 118:16. We must learn something from the things we read! Meditation is an active pursuit, and not just memory.

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-11-17
item.description: :The Psalter. Meditation on Psalm 118:04. The commandments are not arbitrary; they have a purpose. What is diligence (it has two parts) and how do we maintain it? Notes for this passage are found here in word or pdf format
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Meditation on Psalm 118:04. The commandments are not arbitrary; they have a purpose. What is diligence (it has two parts) and how do we maintain it? Notes for this passage are found here in word or pdf format

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-11-24
item.description: :The Psalter. A meditation on Psalm 118:05, "Would that my ways were directed to keep Thy statutes." How does a Christian properly "wish" for something? What must we do to be ready for the change to happen?
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. A meditation on Psalm 118:05, "Would that my ways were directed to keep Thy statutes." How does a Christian properly "wish" for something? What must we do to be ready for the change to happen?

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-12-02
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:06: Then shall I not be ashamed, when I look on all Thy commandments. Shame gives way to illumination.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:06: Then shall I not be ashamed, when I look on all Thy commandments. Shame gives way to illumination.

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-12-02
item.description: :The Psalter. Commentary on Psalm 118:07. There are two kinds of confession of God. How do we learn to do the one that is righteous? Also the Christian understanding of the judgments of God.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Commentary on Psalm 118:07. There are two kinds of confession of God. How do we learn to do the one that is righteous? Also the Christian understanding of the judgments of God.

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-12-15
item.description: :The Psalter. Psalm 118:08 introduces a difficult concept: the grace of God (His abiding presence) may depart from the soul for a season, for many different reasons, known only to God, for the strengthening of the soul, but His mercy is never absent. Examples of this process are seen clearly in the lives of the Prophet Elias and Apostle Peter. The courageous Christian embraces the inevitability that He will chasten whom He loveth, although, being of weak flesh, even the courageous need reassurance. This is not disbelief, but rather a part of our human condition.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Psalm 118:08 introduces a difficult concept: the grace of God (His abiding presence) may depart from the soul for a season, for many different reasons, known only to God, for the strengthening of the soul, but His mercy is never absent. Examples of this process are seen clearly in the lives of the Prophet Elias and Apostle Peter. The courageous Christian embraces the inevitability that He will chasten whom He loveth, although, being of weak flesh, even the courageous need reassurance. This is not disbelief, but rather a part of our human condition.

item.title: The Psalter, 2012-12-29
item.description: :The Psalter. A meditation on Psalm 118:09, "Wherewithal shall a young man correct his way? By keeping Thy words." for something? This is a process, and not cicular reasoning. The Christian law of attraction.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. A meditation on Psalm 118:09, "Wherewithal shall a young man correct his way? By keeping Thy words." for something? This is a process, and not cicular reasoning. The Christian law of attraction.

item.title: The Psalter, 2013-01-27
item.description: :The Psalter. A meditation on Psalm 118:10, "With my whole heart have I sought after Thee, cast me not away from Thy commandments." How should we understand absolute statements that are false? They are a plea for help to make the statement true. Also a short discussion of the use of hyperbole in our prayers.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. A meditation on Psalm 118:10, "With my whole heart have I sought after Thee, cast me not away from Thy commandments." How should we understand absolute statements that are false? They are a plea for help to make the statement true. Also a short discussion of the use of hyperbole in our prayers.

item.title: The Psalter, 2013-02-02
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:11, part of our series. This verse is a promise. When the heart treasures the things of God, there is great power against sin and for virtue. We look to the Theotokos as an example.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:11, part of our series. This verse is a promise. When the heart treasures the things of God, there is great power against sin and for virtue. We look to the Theotokos as an example.

item.title: The Psalter, 2013-02-09
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:12: "Blessed art Thou, O Lord, teach me Thy statutes" The preceding verses are a promise and prerequisite before God will agree to teach us His statues, that is, reveal Himself to us by grace. We must promise to seek after the Lord with all our heart, and desire to treasure all we learn of Him in our heart, and then, God will be attracted to our zeal, and visit us, and teach us His statutes. It is significant that we sing this verse several times in the Evlogitaria of the Resurrection (sung in Sunday matins). Following the reliable principle that the New Testament (and the church, whose life is described in it) interprets the Old, we must therefore live in the resurrection if we are to "entice" God to visit us and teach us about Himself. These words are radical for many, but well understood by true Christian ascetics. Read this psalm daily, and try to live according to it, and you will also understand.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:12: "Blessed art Thou, O Lord, teach me Thy statutes" The preceding verses are a promise and prerequisite before God will agree to teach us His statues, that is, reveal Himself to us by grace. We must promise to seek after the Lord with all our heart, and desire to treasure all we learn of Him in our heart, and then, God will be attracted to our zeal, and visit us, and teach us His statutes. It is significant that we sing this verse several times in the Evlogitaria of the Resurrection (sung in Sunday matins). Following the reliable principle that the New Testament (and the church, whose life is described in it) interprets the Old, we must therefore live in the resurrection if we are to "entice" God to visit us and teach us about Himself. These words are radical for many, but well understood by true Christian ascetics. Read this psalm daily, and try to live according to it, and you will also understand.

item.title: The Psalter, 2013-02-16
item.description: :The Psalter. "With my lips have I declared all the judgments of Thy mouth" is the 13th verse of Psalm 118. It is a superlative statement, and therefore, when we say it we are liars, but God loves our sweet sadness, repentance, and expectant longing as we make an earnest plea in the form of a statement of fact. Only Jesus Christ can say this verse with authority, and in fact, this Psalm describes Him, who certainly declared all the judgments from the Fathers mouth.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. "With my lips have I declared all the judgments of Thy mouth" is the 13th verse of Psalm 118. It is a superlative statement, and therefore, when we say it we are liars, but God loves our sweet sadness, repentance, and expectant longing as we make an earnest plea in the form of a statement of fact. Only Jesus Christ can say this verse with authority, and in fact, this Psalm describes Him, who certainly declared all the judgments from the Fathers mouth.

item.title: The Psalter, 2013-03-16
item.description: :The Psalter. "In the way of Thy testimonies have I found delight, as much as in all riches." a meditation on Psalm 118:14. We must understand what the "way" is and what the testimonies of God are. The Psalmist is describing the constant comparisons we must be making, and the remembrance of death. This cannot understood by study; it is only understood by experience.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. "In the way of Thy testimonies have I found delight, as much as in all riches." a meditation on Psalm 118:14. We must understand what the "way" is and what the testimonies of God are. The Psalmist is describing the constant comparisons we must be making, and the remembrance of death. This cannot understood by study; it is only understood by experience.

item.title: The Psalter, 2013-03-30
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:15. How does one "ponder"? This is a learned skill, and like all skills, requires frequent work and some planning.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:15. How does one "ponder"? This is a learned skill, and like all skills, requires frequent work and some planning.

item.title: The Psalter, 2014-11-08
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:17 Third octave. Gimmel = camel. Pack animal with the most endurance. All about endurance in struggle.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:17 Third octave. Gimmel = camel. Pack animal with the most endurance. All about endurance in struggle.

item.title: The Psalter, 2014-11-22
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:18. the plaintive cry of a sinner who knows his sins make him ignorant and forgetful, and only God can save him from this.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:18. the plaintive cry of a sinner who knows his sins make him ignorant and forgetful, and only God can save him from this.

item.title: The Psalter, I Am A Sojourner On The Earth Hide Not From Me Thy Commandments, 2014-11-29
item.description: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:19. A sojourner has no interest in the places he passes through. The remembrance of death. Ignorance.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. Homily on Psalm 118:19. A sojourner has no interest in the places he passes through. The remembrance of death. Ignorance.

item.title: The Psalter, 2014-12-06
item.description: :The Psalter. The judgments of God are his ways, His nature, not His commandments. We were not born to follow God's commandments; we were born to know God, and following His commandments is essential to know Him. Knowing Him is only possible thorough Him revealing Himself to us, and the more holy the soul, the more God is understood. But we are not holy. This is why the psalmist cries out with great desire "My soul hath ***longed*** to desire Thy judgments at all times"
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. The judgments of God are his ways, His nature, not His commandments. We were not born to follow God's commandments; we were born to know God, and following His commandments is essential to know Him. Knowing Him is only possible thorough Him revealing Himself to us, and the more holy the soul, the more God is understood. But we are not holy. This is why the psalmist cries out with great desire "My soul hath ***longed*** to desire Thy judgments at all times"

item.title: The Psalter, 2014-12-13
item.description: :The Psalter. The Psalmist David says words that must be our own: "Thou hast rebuked the proud; cursed are they that decline from Thy commandments." He is begging to be delivered from his pride.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :The Psalter. The Psalmist David says words that must be our own: "Thou hast rebuked the proud; cursed are they that decline from Thy commandments." He is begging to be delivered from his pride.
